Class AddAppointmentForm

java.lang.Object
com.ogborn.c868final.Controller.AddAppointmentForm
All Implemented Interfaces:
javafx.fxml.Initializable
public class AddAppointmentForm extends Object implements javafx.fxml.Initializable
controls my add appointment/update appointment forms

  • Method Details

    • onUpdateButtonClick

      public void onUpdateButtonClick(javafx.event.ActionEvent event) throws Exception
      Handles the action of clicking the update button, validating the form data, and adding or updating the appointment.
      Parameters:
      event - the action event triggered by the button click
      Throws:
      Exception - if there is an issue during the process

    • onCancelButtonClick

      public void onCancelButtonClick(javafx.event.ActionEvent actionEvent)
      closes back out of the form with no changes
      Parameters:
      actionEvent - the event to be acted upon

    • loadAppointment

      public void loadAppointment(Appointment appointment)
      Loads appointment data into the form for editing and renames the header and update button.
      Parameters:
      appointment - The appointment to load.

    • initialize

      public void initialize(URL url, ResourceBundle resourceBundle)
      Initializes my AddAppointmentForm This method uses a lambda expression to add a listener to the start time ComboBox. The lambda is a concise way to define the behavior that triggers whenever the selected start time changes. Specifically, it updates the end time options dynamically based on the new start time selection. The lambda expression `startTimeComboBox.valueProperty().addListener((observable, oldValue, newValue) -> { ... })`: - Captures changes to the selected value of the start time ComboBox. - Executes the provided block of code whenever a new value (new start time) is selected. - Ensures that the end time ComboBox is repopulated with valid time slots based on the updated start time.
      Specified by:
      initialize in interface javafx.fxml.Initializable
